Operation 'convertHtmlToPdf' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too vague and lacks specificity about inputs, outputs, and use cases. The phrase 'Supports the same parameters as the GET endpoint' is unclear for AI agents without explicit parameter documentation. Improved description clarifies what inputs are accepted (raw HTML or URL in request body), explicitly lists supported features for consistency with GET endpoint, specifies the output (PDF document), and explains the operational advantage (handling large payloads). This helps AI agents understand when to choose POST over GET.

data.current_summary: Convert HTML or URL to PDF via POST
data.current_description: Converts HTML content or a URL to PDF via POST request. Supports the same parameters as the GET endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: Converts HTML content or a URL to PDF via POST request. Accepts raw HTML or URL input in the request body and supports the same conversion options as the GET endpoint (page dimensions, headers/footers, watermarks, security settings, etc.). Returns a PDF document. This endpoint is useful for large HTML payloads or when URL parameters would exceed size limits.
